summary: Backend validation of new EfficientNetB0 binary nail model + clinical features blend + no-auto-login register. 10/11 pytest pass against localhost. Frontend E2E BLOCKED by a critical URL mismatch: frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://mobile-anemia-check.preview.emergentagent.com but the actual ingress responds at https://2ec88571-f9db-4c56-acfa-fc5ea3ec0cf9.preview.emergentagent.com — mobile-anemia-check returns 404 from Cloudflare so every browser API call (register/login/auth/me) fails with CORS/Network error and the UI is unusable end-to-end.

critical.issue: /app/backend/ml/models/eye_mobilenetv2.h5 starts with bytes 'PK\x03\x04' (it is a Keras .keras zip saved under the .h5 name). tensorflow.keras.models.load_model with .h5 extension throws 'Unable to synchronously open file (file signature not found)' and get_eye_model() returns None. As a result every eye screening silently falls back to engine='gemini-fallback' and never produces gradcam_heatmap_base64 from the real CNN. admin/models still reports eye_loaded=true because it only checks file existence.

minor.issue: models_status() reports eye_loaded/nail_loaded based on file existence only, not on a successful load. It should report the cached _EYE_MODEL/_NAIL_MODEL state.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend served at https://2ec88571-f9db-4c56-acfa-fc5ea3ec0cf9.preview.emergentagent.com tries to fetch https://mobile-anemia-check.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) → Cloudflare 404 + CORS error. Register submit shows generic 'Something went wrong. Please try again.' and no row is created. Could not verify register-success-banner, email pre-fill on /login, /screen 'How it works' sidebar, or Results page changes from the browser.

issues: Footer still reads 'CNN · Grad-CAM · Fusion' — given the request to use plain-English labels, the 'CNN' and 'Grad-CAM' tokens here may need rewording too (minor / out of scope of the specific request).

action_items: FIX URL MISMATCH: either update frontend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to https://2ec88571-f9db-4c56-acfa-fc5ea3ec0cf9.preview.emergentagent.com OR remap the mobile-anemia-check.preview.emergentagent.com hostname to this container. Without this, the entire UI is broken in the browser.

Replace /app/backend/ml/models/eye_mobilenetv2.h5 (currently a .keras zip with wrong extension) with either a real HDF5 file OR rename to eye_mobilenetv2.keras AND update EYE_MODEL_PATH to auto-pick .keras like NAIL_MODEL_PATH already does. Currently eye flows silently fall back to gemini-fallback and the new Grad-CAM ROI masking work is never exercised.

After URL/eye-model fixes are in, retest the full frontend flow described in the review request (register → /login banner → email pre-fill → /screen sidebar copy → nail-only Results page hides nail-class-probs and shows nail-clinical-signs).
critical_code_review_comments: ml/config.py auto-resolves NAIL_MODEL_PATH to .keras when present (good). EYE_MODEL_PATH does NOT do the same — it is hardcoded to .h5. Apply the same _KERAS/_H5 fallback for the eye model to avoid the exact corruption-style bug seen here.

ml/model_loader.models_status() should reflect actual load success, not just file existence. Otherwise /api/admin/models is misleading: it shows eye_loaded=true while the in-memory model is None and every request silently degrades.

server.py /api/auth/register now correctly omits the token and skips _set_auth_cookie — matches the requirement. Backend test 'test_register_does_not_auto_login' confirms no token in body and no access_token cookie set. Good.

ml/inference.predict_nail blends model 0.60 + features 0.40 and returns full p_model_anemia / p_features_anemia / clinical_features / feature_weights / blend_weights. NAIL_ANEMIA_INDEX=1 inversion is in place. Confirmed via polarity test: pale (235,215,215) p_features≈0.32, red (200,80,90) p_features≈0.18, pale risk% ≥ red risk%.
ml/pipeline._analyze_nail_sync drops class_probs/top_class/top_prob from extras. Test verified none of these forbidden keys appear anywhere in nail_result.

Grad-CAM still produces a non-empty heatmap_base64 and gradcam_layer for the new EfficientNetB0 nail model (verified in test_nail_only_new_pipeline). The eye Grad-CAM path could not be verified end-to-end because eye_mobilenetv2.h5 won't load (see HIGH issue).
ml/nail_features.combine_nail_signals weights are correctly (0.60, 0.40); feature weights dict sums to 1.0; all 6 clinical features present in response.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_anemia_backend.py (rewritten for new binary nail pipeline: no class_probs, register no-auto-login, polarity sanity check)

success_rate.backend: 91% (10/11)  — only the eye-only Grad-CAM test failed due to corrupted eye model file, not the new code
success_rate.frontend: 0% (BLOCKED by URL/CORS issue — could not exercise any FE flow in the browser)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@anemiacheck.app / Admin@123. Fresh TEST_user_<uuid>@example.com / Passw0rd! created per run.
seed_data_creation: pytest creates ~3 TEST_-prefixed users (register flow + duplicate test + non-admin) and 4 short-lived screenings (nail-only, eye-only, red, pale). Screenings are deleted at end of each test. Users remain (prefix='TEST_').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_anemia_backend.py — 11 tests, ~35s. Run: cd /app/backend/tests && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=http://localhost:8001 python -m pytest -v --tb=short. (External URL https://mobile-anemia-check.preview.emergentagent.com returns 404 — use http://localhost:8001 internally or https://2ec88571-f9db-4c56-acfa-fc5ea3ec0cf9.preview.emergentagent.com externally until the env var is fixed.) Nail model file at /app/backend/ml/models/nail_efficientnetb0.keras (20.08MB) loads OK, output_shape=(None,1). Eye model file eye_mobilenetv2.h5 is actually a Keras zip (PK header) and FAILS to load — fix by renaming to .keras AND/OR adding _KERAS fallback in ml/config.EYE_MODEL_PATH like nail does.
rca of the issue: Eye Grad-CAM regression RCA: ml/config.py only resolves the nail model with the new _KERAS-or-_H5 fallback. The eye model file on disk has the same 'saved as .keras under .h5 extension' issue (first bytes 'PK\x03\x04' = ZIP, not HDF5). tensorflow.keras.models.load_model uses the file extension to pick the reader; with .h5 it goes through h5py which immediately errors 'Unable to synchronously open file (file signature not found)'. The exception is caught in model_loader._load and the function returns None, so pipeline.analyze_eye raises EYE_MODEL_MISSING and server.py silently degrades to the Gemini fallback (engine='gemini-fallback'), which never returns gradcam_heatmap_base64. Fix: rename file to eye_mobilenetv2.keras and add the same _KERAS fallback to EYE_MODEL_PATH in ml/config.py. Frontend RCA: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in frontend/.env points to a hostname that the current k8s ingress does not serve (mobile-anemia-check vs the actual 2ec88571-...); browser hits Cloudflare 404 + cross-origin, so every API call fails. Fix at the env / ingress level — testing agent must not modify PROTECTED frontend/.env.
